schema.name: pm.requirements
schema.description: Requirements for the Pytagoras Fullstack Template. Requirements here are the developer-facing capabilities the template must provide out of the box.

stakeholderNeeds.description: A developer cloning the template should have a fully working fullstack app running locally within 5 minutes.
The template should serve as the foundation for HQ (mission control) and the LMS project — it must be clean enough to build on top of immediately.
Developers evaluating the template should be able to see working AI chat, auth, and admin patterns without writing any code.

requirements.name: Guest chat (unauthenticated)
requirements.statement: The template shall provide a working guest chat interface at the root route that calls an AI model via OpenRouter without requiring authentication.

verification.criteria: Navigate to / — chat input visible, send a message, receive an AI response.

requirements.name: Authenticated chat with history
requirements.statement: The template shall provide authenticated chat with persistent conversation history managed via the database.

verification.criteria: Signed-in user can create, continue, and delete conversations. History persists on page reload.

requirements.name: Real-time streaming AI responses
requirements.statement: AI responses shall stream character-by-character using SSE, with a typing indicator while the response is generating.

verification.criteria: Send a message — response appears incrementally, typing indicator shows during generation.

requirements.name: Theme and language settings
requirements.statement: The template shall allow users to switch between light/dark themes and English/Norwegian languages, with preferences persisted to the database.

verification.criteria: Toggle theme — applies immediately and persists on reload. Switch language — all UI strings change.

requirements.name: Admin panel with user and model management
requirements.statement: The template shall provide an admin-only panel for managing users (roles), system config, and AI models (enable/disable, set default).

verification.criteria: Admin user can access /admin. Non-admin is redirected. Models can be toggled and reordered.

requirements.name: AI model selection per conversation
requirements.statement: Users shall be able to select which AI model to use from a dropdown populated by enabled models in the database.

verification.criteria: Model dropdown shows enabled models. Selected model is used for the conversation.

requirements.name: One-command local setup
requirements.statement: docker-compose up shall start the local db, migrate, backend, and frontend services successfully with no manual steps beyond copying .env files.

verification.criteria: Clone repo, copy .env examples, run docker-compose up — both services healthy within 60 seconds.

requirements.name: All linters and type checkers pass
requirements.statement: The template shall pass biome check (frontend), ruff check (backend), and mypy (backend) with zero errors.

verification.criteria: GitHub Actions CI passes on main branch.

requirements.name: Railway deployment
requirements.statement: The template shall deploy to Railway as two GitHub-linked services (frontend + backend) from the monorepo using railway.toml configs and a standalone uv environment sync script backed by Railway CLI and Neon CLI.

verification.criteria: Frontend and backend Railway services are configured from the GitHub monorepo, the env sync script provisions production and staging variables, and both service health endpoints return 200.

requirements.name: Vertical slice architecture
requirements.statement: All features shall be organised as vertical slices (landing, dashboard, profile, admin) in both frontend and backend, not by technical layer.

verification.criteria: Code review confirms slice isolation. No cross-slice imports at feature level.
